subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Understand that the Earth's surface is constantly moving and changing
Identify the difference between Endogenic (internal) and Exogenic (external) forces
Learn about the basic causes and effects of Earthquakes and Volcanoes
Identify the major landforms created by the work of a River
Recognize how Sea Waves shape the coastline
Understand the role of Ice (Glaciers) in changing the landscape
Learn how Wind creates unique landforms in deserts

lesson_plan.title: Earth Movements: Internal Forces
topics_covered: Lithospheric plates and their slow movement
Volcanoes: A vent in the Earth's crust
teaching_strategy: Analogy: Floating paper pieces in a bowl of water
Simple diagram of a Volcano (Vent, Crater, Magma)
Visual aids showing a 'Vibrating' Earth
student_activities: Draw and label a simple Volcano diagram
Color a map showing major plates (Simplified)
assessment: Oral quiz: What is a Volcano?
Identify the difference between Endogenic and Exogenic

lesson_plan.title: Earthquakes: When the Ground Shakes
topics_covered: Focus and Epicenter of an earthquake
teaching_strategy: Demonstration: Shaking a tray of sand with blocks on it
Discussing the Bhuj earthquake story (NCERT case)
Practice drill: 'Drop, Cover, and Hold on'
student_activities: Mark the Focus and Epicenter on a diagram
List 3 safe spots during an earthquake
assessment: Matching: Instrument vs. Use (Seismograph - measuring vibrations)

lesson_plan.title: Work of a River - Part 1
teaching_strategy: Video/Animation of a river flow
Drawing 'S' shaped curves (Meanders) on the board
Picture analysis: From Waterfall to Lake
student_activities: Draw a Meander and show how it turns into an Ox-bow lake
Observe a picture of Victoria Falls or Angel Falls
assessment: Identifying landforms in a river diagram

lesson_plan.title: Work of a River - Part 2
teaching_strategy: Analogy: A fan-shaped Delta
Discussion on how silt helps farming on floodplains
Mapping the mouth of a river
student_activities: Draw a Delta at the end of a river path
Label the parts of a river system
assessment: Short answer: What is a Delta?

lesson_plan.title: Work of Sea Waves
teaching_strategy: Sequential drawing: Cave $ightarrow$ Arch $ightarrow$ Stack
Using pictures of the coastline
Storytelling: 'How the wave broke the wall'
student_activities: Draw the 3 stages: Sea Cave, Arch, and Stack
Identify which part of a cliff is a Stack
assessment: Matching: Wave action vs. Result (Deposition - Beach)

lesson_plan.title: Work of Ice and Wind
topics_covered: Mushroom Rocks, Sand Dunes, and Loess
teaching_strategy: Analogy: Ice-cream scoop (Glacial hollows)
Drawing a 'Mushroom' shaped rock on the board
Showing pictures of deserts (Loess in China)
student_activities: Sketch a Mushroom Rock and a Sand Dune
List 2 things made by wind in a desert

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Summary
topics_covered: Recap of Endogenic vs. Exogenic forces
Recap of all major landforms (River, Sea, Ice, Wind)
teaching_strategy: Fun game: 'Name the Agent' (e.g., 'Who made the Delta?')
Reviewing NCERT back-exercises
Doubt clearing
assessment: Evaluation of the final summary chart

teaching_aids: Pictures of Earthquakes, Deltas, and Mushroom Rocks
evaluation_methods: Matching the landform to its creator (River/Wind/Ice)
Oral participation in earthquake safety discussion
teacher_tip: To help students remember Sea Caves, Arches, and Stacks, use your hand. A 'C' shape for the cave, a hole through the middle for the arch, and one finger standing alone for the stack. It makes the sequence very easy to visualize!
